title: FCC settles spoofed AI-generated robocalls case against Lingo Telecom
paper_use: authoritative disposition-stage telecom-enforcement row closing the strongest shard-007 synthetic-voice lifecycle cluster with direct penalty and compliance-plan text
dashboard_hook: telecom-enforcement lifecycle node linking rule, NAL, and settlement stages in the provenance/confusion cluster graph
cautious_summary: Direct FCC public texts now support a bounded settlement-stage description: the FCC announced and ordered a consent-decree resolution with Lingo Telecom over spoofed AI-generated robocalls in the New Hampshire primary context, resolved the prior NAL, imposed a $1,000,000 civil penalty, and required a compliance plan tied to STIR/SHAKEN, KYC, and KYUP controls. This supports disposition-stage procedural and control-posture claims, not generalized prevalence or broad technical-effectiveness conclusions.
negative_result_note: The FCC settlement and order are now directly captured, but the New Hampshire DOJ origin-side releases remain blocked from this server and the state case still lacks a directly retrieved canonical source text in the local package.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Compare settlement-stage FCC controls with the still-blocked New Hampshire state-origin releases, or add one second non-telecom document-grade shard-007 family rather than widening with weak multilingual leads.
promotion_note: Promoted on 2026-06-07 after direct capture of FCC release DOC-404951A1 and consent order DA-24-790A1.

title: AG Henry Joins Warning Letter to Company Impersonating President Biden Using A.I. to Spread Misinformation through Robocalls
source_name: PA Office of Attorney General public mirror
paper_use: authoritative U.S. governance-response exemplar for shard-007 discussion of synthetic-voice impersonation, election misinformation, and caller/issuer attribution
dashboard_hook: synthetic-voice case bundle with page/document/federal-policy support badges
cautious_summary: A publicly reachable Pennsylvania OAG mirror page states that AI-generated robocalls impersonating President Biden allegedly contained inaccurate election information, that a 51-attorney-general task force warned Life Corp, and that the calls were allegedly spoofed to appear from legitimate New Hampshire political party numbers; the case should still be cited as an institutional-response record rather than as proof of all underlying facts beyond the cited sources.
negative_result_note: Direct NH DOJ and FCC primary pages for adjacent title-only queue rows were still not captured from this lane, so this record strengthens the U.S. response cluster without claiming full cross-agency completeness.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Extract a narrow document-level summary from the official warning-letter PDF and, if feasible, add one exact FTC quotation or rulemaking sentence to a paper fragment without overclaiming finality.
